references.title: Dynamic Key-Value Memory Networks With Rich  Features for Knowledge Tracing

extracted.Flaw of Previous Models: DKVMN, while interesting as an approach, has the issue of not considering additional features relative to sequences of answers - the knowledge status.
extracted.Novelty of Model: The authors propose a small change to DKVMN that not only includes sequences of answersa to create both matrices, but also considers additional behavioral information in order to obtain a better value matrix.
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
The model starts with the prediction section of whether a question will be correct or not.

This is followed by a Clustering module that is meant to calculate learning ability and group students into one of 3 groups.
extracted.Models: BKT, DKT, DKVMN, DKT-F, DKT-DSC, DKVMN-LA
extracted.Comments:
Authors perform 4 distinct experiments:

1. Comparison od DKVMN-LA with other models in the literature.

2. Comparison of DKVMN-LA with DKT-LA.

3. Analysis of the model suing different segment lengths.

4. Changes with different feature splicings.

DKVMN-LA wiuth all features and segment length 5 outperforms all others.
extracted.Limitations:
Limited number of Benchmark Datasets.

Room for improvement in behavioral features being considered. 

Opportunity for refinement both at the stage of correctedness prediction AND LEarning Ability clustering.

annotations.text: Out of all models in KT that existed in 2022, Dynamic Key-Value Memory Networks (DKVMN) have been argued to be state-of-the-art. A drawback of these models is that they seem to not consider learning abilities or behavioral features collected by ITS.
Thus, to include these theoretically important constructs, the authors propose a new form of representation that improves the performance of KT using DKVMN.
